noveltyJustification: 3 wholly new findings (P0-10 Listener default backlog=1, P0-11 accept-thread decoupling, P0-12 CPU-spike attribution) + 1 partially new (P0-13 active socket inventory) + 1 wholly new (P1-4 zero existing daemon tests) + 1 partially new (P2-4 backlog kwarg uniform across families)

title: multiprocessing.connection.Listener defaults backlog=1; daemon.py:619 inherits this
evidence: Listener.__init__(self, address=None, family=None, backlog=1, authkey=None) per stdlib; daemon.py:619 passes no backlog kwarg. SocketListener calls self._socket.listen(backlog) with default=1.
recommendation: NEW Patch 7: pass backlog=128 (or socket.SOMAXCONN) explicitly. Family-uniform — applies to AF_UNIX and AF_PIPE.

title: Accept thread is decoupled from BrokenPipeError loop
evidence: _accept_loop runs in threading.Thread(daemon=True). Each iter is listener.accept() + asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe (non-blocking). handle_connection (with the buggy send_bytes) runs as asyncio task on the event loop, completely decoupled from accept thread.
recommendation: Q9 verdict: backlog is NOT the bottleneck for the CPU spike. P0-10 is a hygiene patch only.

title: CPU spike root cause: double logger.exception per disconnect (90%) + reconnect storm (10%)
evidence: 564 BrokenPipeError events × 2 logger.exception calls each (one at :438 'streaming response', one at :445 outer 'handling connection') = 1128 traceback formats. grep -cE '^File|Traceback|^\s+at' daemon.log = 2293 traceback lines, ~4 per format. This is the dominant CPU consumer during multi-dispatch storms.
recommendation: Patch 2 (BrokenPipeError-safe wrapper) eliminates ~90% of the spike. Q9 closed.

title: PID 24938 still alive and bound to socket at iter-4 wall-clock
evidence: lsof shows PID 24938 with 4+ open unix-socket fds. Socket file mtime May 1 17:27 unchanged since iter 1. Confirms only one re-bind has occurred since the iter-1 forensics window.
recommendation: No code change. Confirms iter-2 P0-2 mechanism — single rebind per stale-daemon event, not a recurring storm.

title: No daemon-lifecycle tests exist
evidence: grep -rn 'start_daemon|ensure_daemon|stop_daemon' tests/ returned ZERO hits. tests/ directory has no daemon coverage at all.
recommendation: Spec.md Files-to-Change rows for test_daemon.py and test_e2e_daemon.py must be CREATE not Modify. iter-3 P0-9 fcntl-flock helper does not collide with any existing platform fixture (none exist).

title: backlog kwarg works uniformly across AF_UNIX and AF_PIPE
evidence: Listener.__init__ accepts backlog as a positional kwarg before family branching. Both SocketListener and PipeListener accept it. No platform-branching needed for Patch 7.
recommendation: Single-line patch suffices — no sys.platform branch.
